name: Over-generalized single-endpoint API with client-trusted validation

planted_issues.description: A single POST /api/v1/process endpoint handles 8 distinct operations (create, read, update, delete, validate, export, archive, bulk_import) differentiated only by a type field. This violates REST conventions, makes authorization granularity difficult, prevents HTTP caching for reads, and produces an enormous switch statement in the handler.
No API versioning strategy is mentioned beyond the /v1 in the URL. There is no discussion of how breaking changes will be handled, how clients will migrate, or what the deprecation policy is.

Server-side validation can be bypassed by clients sending `options.validated: true`. This trusts the client to have performed validation correctly, which is a security and data integrity violation — any malicious or buggy client can skip validation entirely.

clean_areas.description: The authentication section is well-specified with token lifecycle, refresh mechanism, and granular role-based access control scopes.
